content: For SPA re-attachment, standard pattern combines a MutationObserver on document.body (subtree:true, childList:true) to detect route-driven DOM swaps, plus a popstate listener for back/forward navigation, both gated behind a pathname-change check to avoid firing on unrelated mutations. WeakSet (keyed by DOM element) is the recommended idempotency guard: check-and-add an element to a WeakSet before attaching listeners to it, so re-running the attach routine on an already-instrumented element is a no-op; WeakSet entries are auto-GC'd when the element is removed, avoiding manual cleanup/memory leaks. General MutationObserver best practice: observe as narrow a subtree as possible, do minimal work in the callback (schedule heavier work via requestIdleCallback/microtask), and always have a disconnect path.
